regulatory_context.value: Article 4 applies to providers and deployers of AI systems, requires context-sensitive AI literacy measures, does not mandate a one-size-fits-all training format, certificate, sector template, or governance structure, and already applies from 2 February 2025 with enforcement beginning in August 2026.

value: Legacy all-companies scope copy is wrong; Article 4 is not a blanket requirement for every EU company
The English-only market-gap claim is not established after finding French and multilingual substitutes
EU-wide remote delivery as a complete solution is unproven without country- and context-specific evidence
The FR/DE/EN positioning is not yet a durable moat because Elements of AI already covers those languages
Any product-specific certificate, documentation, or governance-artifact value remains unverified beyond the Commission's baseline

target_audience_summary.value: EU providers and deployers of AI systems, especially HR L&D, compliance, and operating teams that need a practical Article 4 literacy layer.
target_audience_summary.omission_reason: This is a positioning synthesis from the regulatory context rather than a direct dashboard-safe fact.

localization_risk.value: The official guidance points toward context-sensitive implementation, so country and use-case adaptation may matter more than the current EU-wide generic framing implies.
localization_risk.omission_reason: This is an analyst interpretation of the surviving evidence, not a direct dashboard field.

independent_operator_examples.name: Elements of AI
independent_operator_examples.evidence: Free multilingual substitute proving public route and competitive pressure.

independent_operator_examples.name: Haufe Akademie / Bitkom Akademie
independent_operator_examples.evidence: Germany-local AI training ecosystem shows commercial supply around the same competence problem.

pricing_or_offer_proof: Current proof shows a real paid substitute landscape and strong Germany-local keyword demand, but not a clean fragmented small-operator market.
value.social_funnel_signal: Facebook search for `ai act compliance` and `ki schulung` returned generic AI groups and mostly irrelevant school-related noise rather than operator-led compliance communities. No useful Facebook funnel signal was confirmed for this lane.
value.takeaway: The compliance problem is clearly monetized, but visible supply is still academy-led rather than long-tail operator-led.
value.why_not_stronger: The market is real, but current validated competitors are mostly broad academies or free public substitutes.

independent_operator_summary.omission_reason: Derived independent-operator signal for prioritization and further market research. Keep it out of customer-facing copy until translated into product-safe language.

universal_eu_company_scope.omission_reason: The Commission guidance contradicts any blanket all-companies scope statement.

product_problem_solution_fit.omission_reason: No reviewed source proves that one remote EU-wide format fully solves buyer needs across contexts.

multilingual_advantage_claim.omission_reason: French and multilingual substitutes already surfaced, so the language-based moat is not yet proven.

product_compliance_artifact_value.omission_reason: The reviewed guidance does not prove independent compliance significance for any B3-issued certificate, documentation pack, or governance artifact.
